About
Talk to an engineer

Electrical Signature Analysis

One signal. Full drivetrain visibility.

Monitor motors, pumps, and driven equipment from the motor control cabinet. No sensors on the asset. No physical access required.

Every AC motor draws current patterns that reflect drivetrain condition. SAM4 reads these patterns at the MCC to detect faults weeks to months before failure.

9 out of 9 faults detected

At Schiphol Airport, SAM4 identified every developing drivetrain fault, including failures missed by vibration monitoring. Read the case study →

See how ESA works →
On this page What ESA is What it detects Legacy MCSA vs SAM4 Validation

The physics

How electrical signals reveal drivetrain faults

Every drivetrain fault changes the motor's electrical signal. A degrading bearing alters the air gap. A blocked impeller shifts the torque profile. A misaligned coupling creates periodic load oscillations.

These changes appear as patterns in current and voltage, captured by CT and VT clamps at the motor control cabinet. No sensors on the asset. No physical access required.

AI models trained on 10,000+ industrial assets compare each motor's signal against known fault signatures and its own baseline. Reliability engineers validate every anomaly before alerts reach your team.

Motor + Drivetrain Faults change mechanical load
Signal Capture (MCC) CT/VT clamps read current and voltage
AI Fault Detection Models trained on 10,000+ assets
Expert Review Engineers validate every anomaly
SAM4 Platform Actionable alerts and diagnostics

The science is only half the story

ESA captures the signal. AI identifies the anomaly. But every alert is reviewed by a Samotics reliability engineer before it reaches your team. They confirm fault type, assess severity, and recommend a specific action. Time-critical alerts are escalated immediately.

See the full service model → See the five-step pipeline →

Detection capability

What ESA detects across the drivetrain

One sensor point per motor. Eight fault categories across five equipment types. The matrix below shows what SAM4 detects today, validated on 10,000+ monitored assets.

Bearing degradation Misalignment Imbalance Cavitation Clogging Electrical faults Process deviations Efficiency loss
Fault type Motors Pumps Fans Gearboxes Compressors
Bearing degradation ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓
Misalignment ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓
Imbalance ✓ ✓ ✓ ● ✓
Cavitation — ✓ — — —
Clogging / blockage — ✓ ● — —
Electrical faults ✓ ✓ ✓ — ✓
Process deviations ● ✓ ● ● ✓
Efficiency degradation ✓ ✓ ✓ ● ●
✓ Detected ● Emerging — N/A

Bearing degradation, misalignment, and electrical faults are detected across all motor-driven equipment. Process-specific faults like cavitation are equipment-dependent.

See real detection examples →

What's different

Same physics. Different experience.

Motor Current Signature Analysis has existed for over 40 years. It works — but it requires a specialist, a scheduled visit, and manual interpretation. SAM4 turns ESA into a continuous, automated service.

Legacy MCSA

  • Monitoring mode Periodic — scheduled visits
  • Operator Specialist with oscilloscope
  • Data interpretation Manual expert analysis
  • Scale One asset at a time
  • Output Report (days–weeks later)
  • Fleet coverage Typically 5–20 critical assets

SAM4 ESA

  • Monitoring mode Continuous — 24/7 automated
  • Operator No specialist required
  • Data interpretation AI models + diagnostic team
  • Scale Entire fleet simultaneously
  • Output Real-time alerts + dashboard
  • Fleet coverage 1,000+ assets per site
800×500 SAM4 vs legacy MCSA comparison Two-column comparison. Left 'Legacy MCSA' (grey): single snapshot, manual analysis, periodic walk-around, current only — each with red X. Right 'SAM4 ESA' (blue): continuous 24/7, AI-automated, remote from MCC, current + voltage — each with blue checkmark.

The physics hasn't changed. The experience has — from a specialist tool to an operational platform your maintenance team can use.

See how ESA compares to vibration monitoring →

Validation

40 years of ESA research. 10,000 assets monitored.

10,000+ assets monitored globally
5,300+ validated fault detections
40+ years of ESA research
80+ industrial customers

Detection performance

95% early fault detection rate. <1% false positive rate. Measured across the full monitored fleet over a 12-month period.

ISO 20958

International standard for electrical signature condition monitoring. SAM4 aligns with the ISO 20958 methodology.

ABB integration

SAM4 ESA is integrated in ABB ACS880 drives. ABB evaluated ESA technologies on the market and selected Samotics.

Peer-reviewed research

ESA methods published in IEEE and EPRI research since the 1980s. The physics is established.

ESA is not a new concept. It is a mature monitoring method now deployed at industrial scale.

See the full technical architecture and performance data →

Honest boundaries

What ESA doesn't do

Not a replacement for vibration on accessible assets

If a sensor can be mounted directly on the asset, vibration offers higher spectral resolution for some bearing and gear faults. ESA is strongest where sensors cannot be installed.

Not real-time protection

ESA detects developing degradation, not sudden catastrophic events like foreign object damage. Typical warning time: weeks to months before failure.

Load and drive topology matter

Detection confidence is highest above ~30% rated load and on standard drive topologies. Some older or non-standard VFDs can reduce signal quality. We assess compatibility during scoping.

Detection rate: ~95%

Across confirmed failures, roughly 1 in 20 did not have a prior SAM4 alert. We publish this because real engineering decisions require real numbers.

New asset types need learning time

Condition, performance, and energy monitoring start immediately. But for asset types SAM4 hasn't seen before, fault detection models need operational data to establish a baseline. First fault alerts may take 4–8 weeks.

“Instead of needing to be placed on the asset in the field, SAM4 installs inside the motor control cabinet, out of harm's way. And its advanced analytics were just what we needed.”

Andy Roegis Industrial Digitalization Manager, ArcelorMittal

Request a demo and speak with one of our reliability engineers. We'll show you what ESA detects on assets like yours.
